reviews.review_msg: The majority of class time was spent either talking about Terry and how awesome he is, or discussing how he should improve the class in the future.  Other than that, the half hour of class spent on material is very useful, and the class prepares you for starting your own business. Would't suggest taking until 4th year.

Terry is one of the most amazing professors that SFU has to offer. He provides an easy to follow guide on how to do well in his class... and while the course wasn't easy... the lessons I learned in this class are the most valuable of any course I took at SFU. Terry genuinely cares about his students and their future success... don't miss this class

Class is interesting but his marking is somethiang amazing. If you got 75%, you grade is D. The difference between B-, B, B+ and A was just one percent or even half of the percent. Also, he has no requirements and grading criterias. You never know why you got this grade.

Looking back on my degree I can say w/ confidence Terry is in my top profs at SFU. You learn a lot about business and startups in lectures and even more from the project (which he breaks down in a pretty foolproof way). Also, as a prof who currently runs a startup, you get a VERY refreshing perspective on entrepreneurship. Highly recommend to all.

Class is very intense. He can make you feel very small. Very fast pace/cut to the chase businessman. In lectures, he's very entertaining. He has an impressive record and flaunts it. More focus on his business rather than the class, but he does care about his students, gives great feedback. Recommended, but you better be tough to handle his critique
Absolutely amazing teacher.  If you're not hooked on entrepreneurship after the first class there is something wrong with you.

He is probably the worst professor in the Faculty of Business at SFU. He spent classes ranting about his own life and was not contactable so your mark basically came out of nowhere and you couldn't question it. Hope SFU gets rid of him soon.

Good intentioned teacher, but lazy and provides little feedback. Interesting at times (and pretty easy class), but you don't really learn anything. Very little support for your project. Nice prof, but wouldn't recommend the course.
